title: Earnings call transcript: Innodata Q4 2025 earnings beat expectations
text: Innodata exceeded financial expectations in Q4 2025, reporting earnings per share of $0.25 against forecasts of $0.22, alongside revenue of $72.38 million versus projected $70.31 million. The company achieved 22% year-over-year revenue growth, bringing full-year 2025 revenue to $251.7 million with 48% annual growth. Financial highlights: Adjusted gross margin reached 42%, surpassing the 40% target. Adjusted EBITDA totaled $15.7 million (22% of revenue). Cash position strengthened to $82.2 million. Stock rose 3.18% in aftermarket trading to $47.66. Strategic focus centered on: CEO Jack Abuhoff emphasized the company's expansion beyond traditional data services into AI lifecycle support, highlighting innovations in: Generative AI model training and long-context reasoning. Autonomous agent evaluation and optimization. Physical AI and robotics applications. Adversarial simulation systems for AI security. 2026 Outlook: Management projects year-over-year growth of approximately 35% or more, representing a deceleration from 2025's 48% growth. The company expects improved customer diversification and margin expansion through automation and synthetic data platforms.
